groups.context_description: Slave of Christ as inner-being identity — defining self-understanding of the apostle and believer as one wholly given over to Christ's service and authority
Slavery to sin and bondage as former or present inner-being condition apart from God
Faithful servanthood as inner-being quality — stewardship, trustworthiness, and humility before the master as inner dispositions
Social and eschatological levelling of slave and free — inner-being equality of all persons before God transcending social status
Inner-being impossibility of divided ultimate loyalty — heart's singular orientation toward one master; service to God and worldly powers mutually exclusive
Slavery to disordered inner desires — state of the person bound in service to passions, pleasures, and appetite rather than God
Willing service as inner-being expression of love, fervency, and Spirit-led freedom — what distinguishes God-directed service from compelled service
Inner bond of shared servanthood — relational identity of those who serve together under the same Lord, characterised by solidarity and common orientation toward God
Slavery to righteousness and God — inner-being reorientation from bondage to sin to willing service of God, producing holiness
Inner bondage to disordered desires — state of the inner person enslaved to passions, pleasures, or substances
Voluntary servanthood for others — inner act of self-subordination in service of another's flourishing
Inner posture of the Lord's servant — willing humble availability to God's word and purpose as defining inner-being self-identification
Act of bringing others into bondage — external imposition of spiritual or relational enslavement that threatens the inner freedom given by Christ
Inner act of bringing the body under disciplined governance — inner will asserting mastery over bodily impulses in service of spiritual integrity
Spiritual slavery as inner-being condition — bondage to fear, law, or death that characterises the person apart from Christ's liberation
Eschatological liberation from bondage — creation and persons set free from corruption into the freedom of God's glory
Term names obedience as the inner disposition of faith — the volitional compliance of the whole person with God's word, resulting in righteousness and purified souls
Term names Christ's obedience as the ground of righteousness for many — the inner-being act of the Son that reverses Adam's disobedience
Term names obedience as the inner orientation of persons toward legitimate authority — whether willing compliance or its contemptuous refusal
Term names submission as an inner-relational orientation — the disposition of yielding to God, the gospel, or legitimate authority, arising from inner conviction and expressed in conduct

Term names voluntary submission as the fundamental inner-relational orientation of the person before God and within human community — placing oneself under God's authority, arising from conscience, reverence, and faith
Closing the heart against compassion — the inner act of hardening whereby the inner being shuts itself off from pity
"By no means!" — strong inner-being moral assertion against misapplication of grace
"Do not say in your heart" and other inner-being directives — mē shaping the inner life
Inner-being absence or incapacity named through negation — no fear of God, cannot see, did not seek
Term names deliberate human action as the expression of inner character and will — what a person does as the outward enactment of their inner being
Term names acquittal or the withholding of punishment — the inner-being state of being declared innocent before God or of God restraining just judgment
"There is hope" / "there is a reward" — existential inner-being anchor in desolation
Inner moral existence and its absence — "is there any righteous?", "there is no fear of God", "lies in the heart"
Consent, willingness, and relational existence — "if you are willing", "the Lord is with us"
"This mystery has been revealed" — de.nah pointing to inner vision, divine disclosure, and the thoughts of the mind
Inner resolve, loyalty, spirit, and judgment — de.nah pointing to inner-being conditions in Daniel and his accusers
Term names the broken spirit as an inner-being condition of contrition — the crushed and humbled heart that God does not despise
Term names the breaking of the inner person by grief, shame, or divine judgment — the shattering of the human spirit under overwhelming weight
Divine betrothal — God's covenantal commitment grounded in inner-being character qualities; the goal: knowing the Lord
Divine commission — "Go!" as the inner-being call to prophetic obedience and vocation
Going after other gods / false loves — inner-being movement toward idolatry and spiritual infidelity
The way you should go — divine guidance of inner direction; God going before or with his people
Going to/seeking God or failing to find him — inner-being orientation toward the divine
Going toward death — inner-being confrontation with mortality and departure
"My heart has gone after my eyes" — inner-being mechanism of moral departure
Term sets a temporal boundary on an inner-being state — marking fury, anger, guilt, endurance, or spiritual renewal as time-bounded, changeable, and having a foreseeable turning point
"How long?" — the inner-being cry of anguish, longing, and tested patience
God enthroned / dwelling in the midst of his people — divine presence as the ground of inner security
Dwelling in darkness / sitting in ashes — inner-being conditions of desolation, lament, and humiliation
Dwelling as eschatological rest and security — the promise of peaceful habitation
Covenantal dwelling — "dwell as mine", "I will dwell in your midst", the intimate possession of dwelling
The arrogant heart's aspiration to sit on high — inner pride named in the will to enthrone oneself
Inner-being grief and solidarity expressed in sitting — sitting in ashes, sitting with the suffering
Inner-being identity and relational status — "I am," "we are," being named in relation to God, sin, grace, the flesh
No one righteous, no one seeks — the universal inner-being condition of humanity
The word is near, in your mouth and in your heart; hope, conviction, calling — inner-being states named through "being"
Eschatological orientation — things to come, the age to come, glory to be revealed — shaping inner hope and expectation
Imminence of consequence or trial — shaping inner response of fear, endurance, or urgent action
Faith acting toward the future — Abraham, Isaac, future blessings
Being willing, content, or pleased — the inner state of consent, determination, or desire governing action
Foolishness as inner-being condition — deluded judgment, sinful folly, and the incapacity to know God's way
The freewill offering — inner-being voluntary devotion expressed in giving, sacrifice, and praise
Term names the noble person as a moral character type — inner dignity and reliability contrasted with the fool and the scoundrel
Term names noble willingness as an inner character quality — the generous disposition that gives freely without compulsion
Term names the nobility of the soul — the inner-being quality of generosity and dignity in their highest expression
Acting of one's own accord — voluntary inner impulse expressed in giving and service
Sect/faction as an expression of chosen inner orientation — religious commitment, communal division, or doctrinal choice
Choosing as inner act of will — divine election and human deliberate choice
Divine appointment — God's will expressed in calling and commissioning persons for purposes
Divine election as inner-being delight — God's chosen servant named with his soul's pleasure
The heart's inner decision as the governing act of will — giving from inner resolve, not compulsion
Divine pre-selection of witnesses — God's choice of those who encounter the risen Christ
Self-will as inner-being vice — arrogant self-assertion and willful presumption
Term names inheritance as a moral-spiritual outcome — what a person receives as the result of their inner character and relationship with God
Term names God as the inheritance of the person — the inner-being orientation of those who possess God himself as their portion
Term names God himself or divine presence as the inheritance of the faithful person — the ultimate inner-being possession
Term names the inheritance of the righteous as a moral-spiritual promise — what the upright receive as the outcome of their inner character
Term names what is received as an inner-being treasure — the law, children, or wisdom as inheritance of the heart
Term used in moral sowing metaphors — sowing righteousness or injustice as inner character dispositions that determine what is reaped
Term used of God's sowing of inner states — light, joy, restoration, re-planting as covenantal love
Term names sowing in tears — grief-laden faithful labour as an inner experience anticipating joyful harvest
Term names patient inner forbearance — bearing-with of the faithless or difficult, at inner cost
Term names spiritual fruit-bearing — the inner productive life emerging from abiding in Christ, requiring pruning and self-surrender
Term names being carried along by the Spirit — the inner experience of prophets borne by the Holy Spirit in inspired speech
Term names divine upholding — Christ bearing all things by the word of his power
Term names God's patient endurance — divine long-suffering borne toward the resistant
Term names the inner bearing of reproach — carrying the shame of Christ as costly discipleship
Term names the inner movement toward maturity — pressing forward to full spiritual development
Term names inner disposition of hope toward coming grace — setting hope fully on what will be brought at Christ's revelation
Term names inner moral integrity of fulfilling sworn commitments to God — weight of conscience under oath
Term names inner sincerity of devotional acts — giving, praying, fasting toward God rather than human approval
Term names moral accountability before God — giving account for words, deeds, stewardship, at final judgment
Term names inner moral discernment of dual obligation — rendering to human authority and to God what each is owed
Term names inner restraint of refusing evil for evil — the moral choice to bless rather than retaliate
Term names inner transformation of repentance expressed through generous voluntary restitution
Term names inner fruit produced by endured discipline — peaceable righteousness formed through training
Term names inner failure of misplaced valuation — selling eternal inheritance for immediate appetite
Term names inner disposition of honouring obligations — giving respect and honour as moral debts
Term names the act of giving testimony — expressing inner conviction through witness
Term names the offspring as bearer of covenantal enmity — primordial hostility between lines establishing inner orientation of antagonism
Term names inner longing for offspring — the grief of childlessness as a deep inner yearning
Term names offspring defined by inner moral character — godly, holy, or corrupt seed whose spiritual quality is their defining mark
Term names the inner transformation of offspring — the Spirit poured on descendants; heart of offspring circumcised to love God
Term names seed as emblem of hope carried through grief — the weeping sower who shall return with joy
Term names seed as metaphor for inner moral character and peace sown as inner-community state
Term used in metaphor of divine sowing — God causing righteousness and praise to sprout; naming inner character qualities he cultivates
Term names vegetables chosen as alternative to defilement — inner moral conviction of refusing to compromise faithfulness to God through dietary integrity
Term names provision given in response to faithfulness — the honouring of moral integrity through the granting of the chosen diet
Term names betrayal — the inner moral act of handing over a trusted person to harm for treacherous or self-interested motives
Term names the passion of Christ — Jesus delivered over by the Father's eternal purpose to suffering and death
Term names Christ's voluntary self-giving — the inner act of love in which Christ gives himself over for others
Term names God's judicial delivering over — abandoning the rebellious to their own inner lusts and debased mind
Term names the apostolic handing on of tradition — the deliberate inner commitment to faithful transmission of received truth
Term names the disciple's inner experience of being delivered up for Christ's sake — requiring trust in divine provision
Term names the entrustment of stewardship — the master's trust in servants and the servant's calling of faithful management
Term names continual inner self-entrustment to God — trusting self-surrender to the one who judges justly under unjust suffering
Term names the inner moral act of giving oneself over to desire — the hardened self-abandonment to sensuality
Term names the divine delegation of all authority — the Father handing over all things to the Son
Term names disciplinary delivering over to Satan — aimed at the inner restoration and salvation of the spirit
Term names the heart's obedience to a received pattern of teaching — the inner act of self-surrender to a shaping tradition
Term in the water/Spirit contrast — water as the outward insufficient sign pointing to inner transformation of Spirit baptism
Term as living water — metaphor for inner spiritual life permanently satisfying inner thirst, welling up to eternal life
Term in the context of inner cleansing — water as instrument of purification touching conscience and moral state
Term associated with inner anguish and desperate craving — the tormented soul's thirst for relief
Term in the act of washing hands as moral self-exculpation — the external act revealing inner conflict over guilt
Term as water of life in eschatological promise — eternal inner satisfaction and refreshment of the redeemed
Term as element of faith-testing — water on which the person either walks by trust or sinks through doubt
Term revealing inner disposition through contrast — water not given (indifference) vs. tears given (love)
Term names the gift as an act of worship — offering given as expression of inner devotion toward God
Term names the moral claim of relational conscience amid worship — the gift interrupted by reconciliation
Term names misuse of religious dedication as pretext for moral evasion — piety masking filial failure
Term names inner inadequacy of ritual gifts to perfect the conscience — gap between external offering and inner transformation
Term names salvation as God's free gift — not earned but received, establishing inner dependence and gratitude
Term names radical self-giving generosity — total offering from poverty as expression of complete inner trust
Term names the divine free gift received by the person — grace, righteousness, calling — that transforms the inner being and establishes standing before God
Term names inner disposition of good giving — a father giving to a son who asks; and God's generous orientation toward those who ask
Term names the giving that designates the betrayer — the final extension of grace in the moment before betrayal
Term names inner self-restrained generosity — giving to those who cannot repay, oriented toward divine reward
Term names the inner relinquishing of personal vengeance — entrusting retribution to God
Term expresses impulse to return thanksgiving — gratitude as inner response to grace
Term names God's just inner commitment to repay — divine retributive justice; impossibility of placing God in debt
Term names inner disposition of radical generosity — distributing to those in need from a transformed orientation toward wealth
Term names surrender of power as expression of inner allegiance — handing authority to the object of devotion
Term names inner disposition of sharing — giving from oneself to another in need from love and reoriented will; extending even to sharing one's very self
Term names God's granting of all things needed for inner transformation — life, godliness, participation in the divine nature
Term names the divine perfect gift — every good gift from above; gift bringing justification where condemnation was due
Term names the impossibility of preceding God in giving — no person can give first so as to put God in debt; establishing pure creaturely dependence
